key_insight: A mixed finite element discretization of the intrinsic Hodges beam equations preserves structure while enabling beam dynamics and multibody assembly without algebraic constraints.
engineering_relevance: This offers a numerically robust alternative for explicit multibody simulation of geometrically exact beams, with natural boundary conditions, closed-loop capability, and energy-preserving time integration.
method: Discretize the intrinsic beam formulation with mixed finite elements to handle linear differential operators directly.
Impose kinematic and dynamic boundary conditions naturally and assemble multibody systems without kinematic constraint equations.
Combine the port-Hamiltonian structure with implicit midpoint integration for exact energy preservation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports demonstration on multiple examples, including closed kinematic loops, and notes fewer Newton iterations than existing energy-preserving schemes.
watch_item: Performance and robustness may depend on careful mixed-element design and time-integration setup, especially for nonlinear interconnected systems.
paper_title: Mixed finite element discretization of intrinsic geometrically exact beams for explicit multibody dynamics
